message:
feat: implement proper transparency compositing and hit grid double-buffering

This commit improves rendering correctness for layer compositing and hit
testing with proper frame isolation.

Cell transparency:
- Add `Cell::transparent()` for true no-op compositing cells
- Transparent cells have Empty content + TRANSPARENT fg/bg
- Unlike `Cell::clear(Rgba::TRANSPARENT)`, these don't tint underlying content

Buffer transparency:
- Add `OptimizedBuffer::clear_transparent_with_pool()` method
- Properly clears to fully transparent state without tinting
- Correctly decrements grapheme pool references

Renderer double-buffering for hit grids:
- Split single `hit_grid` into `front_hit_grid` and `back_hit_grid`
- Hit registrations accumulate in back buffer during frame
- Swap buffers on present() for proper frame isolation
- hit_test() now reads from front (last presented frame)
- Fixes race conditions between registration and testing

Layer initialization:
- Layers now start fully transparent on creation
- Resize clears layers to transparent state
- Use `clear_transparent_with_pool()` instead of clear with TRANSPARENT

Test improvements:
- Replace `_ => panic!(...)` with `other => assert!(matches!(...))`
- Provides better error messages on test failure
- Add `commit_hits_for_test()` helper for hit testing
- Update tests to call commit before asserting hit results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24f","title":"ANSI Cursor Color","description":"## Overview
Add OSC 12 cursor color sequence.

## Scope
```rust
// In src/ansi/sequences.rs
pub fn cursor_color(r: u8, g: u8, b: u8) -> String {
    format!("\x1b]12;#{:02x}{:02x}{:02x}\x07", r, g, b)
}

pub const CURSOR_COLOR_RESET: &str = "\x1b]112\x07";
```

```rust
impl Terminal {
    pub fn set_cursor_color(&mut self, color: Rgba) -> io::Result<()>;
    pub fn reset_cursor_color(&mut self) -> io::Result<()>;
}
```

## Files to Modify
- src/ansi/sequences.rs - add cursor color function
- src/terminal/mod.rs - add methods

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (minimum 2 tests)
- test_cursor_color_sequence
- test_cursor_color_reset

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sequence format is correct
- [ ] Color conversion works
- [ ] Unit tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Sequence format is correct
- [ ] Color conversion works
- [ ] Unit tests pass

 {"id":"bd-24kr","title":"Implement set_truncate_mode(mode) - Line truncation mode","description":"# Task: Implement set_truncate_mode()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn set_truncate_mode(&mut self, mode: TruncateMode)
```

## TruncateMode Enum
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum TruncateMode {
    #[default]
    None,       // No truncation, horizontal scroll
    Ellipsis,   // Show '...' at end of truncated line
    FadeOut,    // Alpha gradient at line end
    Hard,       // Hard cut, no indicator
}
```

## Purpose
Controls how lines longer than the view width are displayed.

## Implementation Notes
1. Store mode in view
2. Affects line rendering logic
3. Ellipsis replaces last 3 chars with '...'
4. FadeOut uses alpha gradient over last N chars

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TruncateMode enum defined
- [ ] Mode affects rendering
- [ ] Each mode renders correctly
- [ ] Default is None

## Testing
- Each truncate mode
- Long line truncation
- Short line (no truncation needed)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:29:31.634947516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:29:31.648267826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24kr","depends_on_id":"bd-1vn5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:29:31.648241738Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-24q2","title":"Subtask: Golden File Test Cases","description":"# Golden File Test Cases

## Scope
Create the actual golden file test cases.

## Test Case Categories

### Basic Rendering (5 cases)
1. empty_buffer_80x24.golden - empty buffer render
2. single_char_center.golden - one character in center
3. full_screen_text.golden - text filling entire screen
4. box_single_line.golden - single-line box
5. box_double_line.golden - double-line box

### Color Rendering (5 cases)
6. truecolor_gradient.golden - RGB gradient
7. color256_palette.golden - 256-color mode output
8. color16_palette.golden - 16-color mode output
9. bold_colors.golden - bold attribute affects color
10. dim_colors.golden - dim attribute effects

### Complex Rendering (5 cases)
11. alpha_blend_50.golden - 50% alpha overlay
12. scissor_clipped.golden - content clipped by scissor
13. nested_scissor.golden - nested scissor regions
14. opacity_stack.golden - opacity stack effects
15. wide_chars_cjk.golden - CJK wide characters

### Unicode (5 cases)
16. emoji_basic.golden - basic emoji
17. emoji_zwj.golden - ZWJ sequences (family emoji)
18. combining_marks.golden - combining diacriticals
19. rtl_text.golden - right-to-left text (if supported)
20. mixed_width.golden - ASCII + CJK mixed

### Demo Showcase (5 cases)
21. tour_screen_1.golden - first tour screen
22. tour_screen_5.golden - middle tour screen
23. help_overlay.golden - F1 help overlay
24. debug_panel.golden - Ctrl+D debug panel
25. command_palette.golden - Ctrl+P palette

## Golden File Format
\`\`\`
# Golden file: empty_buffer_80x24
# Generated: 2026-01-28
# Terminal: xterm-256color
# Size: 80x24
---
<raw ANSI output>
\`\`\`

## Files to Create
- tests/golden/*.golden - golden files
- tests/golden/README.md - format documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- 25 golden files created
- All scenarios covered
- Update script available
- Format documented","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:43.532418893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:35:22.444918753Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:35:22.444790775Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24q2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ghx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:31.784474811Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24q2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rov","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:43.539250810Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-25w","title":"Evaluate Event System Enhancement","description":"## Overview
Evaluate whether the current event system needs enhancement or if it's sufficient as-is.

## Already Implemented (NO WORK NEEDED)
In `src/event.rs`:
- `LogLevel` enum (Debug, Info, Warn, Error) ✓
- `set_event_callback()` - global event callback ✓
- `emit_event()` - emit events to callback ✓
- `set_log_callback()` - global log callback ✓
- `emit_log()` - emit log messages ✓
- Thread-safe via `Mutex<Option<...>>` ✓
- Tests for both callbacks ✓

## Current Design
The current implementation uses **global static callbacks** with:
```rust
type EventCallback = Box<dyn Fn(&str, &str) + Send + Sync + 'static>;
type LogCallback = Box<dyn Fn(LogLevel, &str) + Send + Sync + 'static>;
```

This is simple and sufficient for most use cases.

## Potential Enhancements (EVALUATE NEED)
The original bead described an `EventBus` struct with:
- Multiple handlers via `subscribe()`/`unsubscribe()`
- `Trace` log level
- Typed events with `Input`, `TerminalResponse`, `Resize`, `Custom` variants

**Questions to Answer:**
1. Do users need multiple event handlers?
2. Is a `Trace` log level needed?
3. Do we need typed events instead of string-based events?
4. Is the current simple design causing any issues?

## Recommendation
**DEFER** this bead unless a specific use case requires the EventBus pattern. The current implementation:
- Is simpler to use
- Has lower overhead
- Is thread-safe
- Works for the common case (single callback)

If multiple handlers are needed, they can be implemented at the application level using the single callback.

## If Enhancement is Needed
Only implement if there's a concrete use case. Minimal changes would be:

```rust
// Add Trace level if needed
pub enum LogLevel {
    Trace,  // ADD
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}
```

## Files to Modify (if needed)
- src/event.rs - Add Trace level (minimal change)

## Testing Requirements (if implemented)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_log_trace_level() {
    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
    
    let messages = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
    let msgs_clone = messages.clone();
    
    set_log_callback(move |level, msg| {
        msgs_clone.lock().unwrap().push((level, msg.to_string()));
    });
    
    emit_log(LogLevel::Trace, "trace message");
    emit_log(LogLevel::Debug, "debug message");
    
    let msgs = messages.lock().unwrap();
    eprintln!("[TEST] Logged messages: {:?}", msgs);
    
    assert_eq!(msgs.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(msgs[0].0, LogLevel::Trace);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Trace level works");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evaluate if current implementation is sufficient
- [ ] If enhancement needed: Add Trace log level
- [ ] If enhancement needed: Tests pass with logging
- [ ] Document decision in this bead

## Status
**Recommend: CLOSE or DEFER** - Current implementation is likely sufficient.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Event handlers can be registered
- [ ] Events dispatch to all registered handlers
- [ ] Log callback works with all levels
- [ ] Thread-safe (Send + Sync)
- [ ] All 8+ unit tests pass

 {"id":"bd-2631","title":"Implement get_fragmentation_ratio() - Memory fragmentation","description":"# Task: Implement get_fragmentation_ratio()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn get_fragmentation_ratio(&self) -> f32
```

## Purpose
Returns the ratio of freed slots to total slots. High ratio suggests compaction may be beneficial.

## Implementation Notes
- ratio = freed_count / total_slots
- Returns 0.0 if empty
- Returns value in [0.0, 1.0]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correct ratio calculation
- [ ] Handles empty pool

## Testing
- Empty pool (0.0)
- No freed slots (0.0)
- Half freed (0.5)
- All freed (would be 1.0, though unusual)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:48.850248551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:26:06.689544114Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:26:06.689481928Z","close_reason":"Implemented get_fragmentation_ratio() method with full test coverage","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2631","depends_on_id":"bd-g3az","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:48.866548366Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
